title: Shopping, Money & Directions
description: Learn how to ask for directions, shop at a mall, and handle money. Navigate a city with confidence.
descriptionTr: Yön tarifi sormayı, bir AVM'de alışveriş yapmayı ve parayla ilgili konuşmayı öğren. Şehirde güvenle dolaş.

vocabulary.example: Turn left at the traffic lights.
vocabulary.extra: You need to turn left after the bank.

vocabulary.example: Turn right at the corner.
vocabulary.extra: Turn right here and you'll see the mall.

vocabulary.example: Go straight for two blocks.
vocabulary.extra: Go straight and the station is on your left.

vocabulary.example: Cross the street at the crosswalk.
vocabulary.extra: Be careful when you cross the road.

vocabulary.example: The pharmacy is on the corner.
vocabulary.extra: Wait for me at the corner of the street.

vocabulary.example: The hotel is two blocks away.
vocabulary.extra: Walk three blocks and you'll find it.

vocabulary.example: Do you pay with cash or card?
vocabulary.exampleTr: Nakit mi yoksa kartla mı ödüyorsun?
vocabulary.extra: I don't have cash. Is there an ATM near here?
vocabulary.extraTr: Nakit yok. Yakınlarda ATM var mı?

vocabulary.example: Can I pay by credit card?
vocabulary.extra: We accept all credit cards.

vocabulary.example: Can I have the receipt, please?
vocabulary.extra: Keep the receipt for the refund.

vocabulary.example: Here is your change — five dollars.
vocabulary.extra: Do you have change for twenty dollars?

vocabulary.example: Is there a discount on this jacket?
vocabulary.extra: Students get a ten percent discount.

vocabulary.example: What size do you wear?
vocabulary.extra: Do you have this in a larger size?
vocabulary.extraTr: Bunun daha büyük bedeni var mı?

vocabulary.example: Can I try on this shirt?
vocabulary.extra: The fitting room is over there if you want to try it on.

vocabulary.example: This dress doesn't fit me. It's too small.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Bu elbise bana olmuyor. Çok küçük.
vocabulary.extra: Does this jacket fit you well?
vocabulary.extraTr: Bu ceket sana iyi oluyor mu?

vocabulary.example: Can I exchange this for a bigger size?
vocabulary.exampleTr: Bunu daha büyük bedenle değiştirebilir miyim?
vocabulary.extra: You can exchange it within 30 days.

vocabulary.example: I'd like a refund, please.
vocabulary.extra: Sorry, we don't give refunds on sale items.

vocabulary.example: Let's go to the mall this weekend.
vocabulary.extra: The mall opens at ten in the morning.

vocabulary.example: Take the escalator to the second floor.
vocabulary.extra: The escalator is next to the elevator.

vocabulary.example: The entrance is on the main street.
vocabulary.extra: Wait for me at the entrance of the mall.

vocabulary.example: Where is the exit?
vocabulary.extra: The emergency exit is at the back.

vocabulary.example: The shoe store is on the first floor.
vocabulary.extra: This building has five floors.

vocabulary.example: The bank is opposite the supermarket.
vocabulary.extra: My house is opposite the park.

vocabulary.example: The café is between the bank and the pharmacy.
vocabulary.extra: Sit between your mom and dad.

vocabulary.example: Walk past the hospital and turn left.
vocabulary.extra: The restaurant is just past the bridge.

vocabulary.example: The bookstore is next to the coffee shop.
vocabulary.extra: Can I sit next to you?

vocabulary.example: The milk is in aisle three.
vocabulary.extra: Go down this aisle and you'll find the shoes.

vocabulary.example: Pay at the cashier near the exit.
vocabulary.extra: The cashier gave me the wrong change.
vocabulary.extraTr: Kasiyer bana yanlış para üstü verdi.

vocabulary.example: There is a big sale at the mall today.
vocabulary.extra: I bought this bag on sale. It was fifty percent off!
vocabulary.extraTr: Bu çantayı indirimde aldım. Yüzde elli indirimdi!

vocabulary.example: You get twenty percent off today.
vocabulary.extra: The tax is eight percent.

vocabulary.example: Your total is forty-five dollars.
vocabulary.extra: What is the total with tax?

grammar.title: Imperatives + How much + Prepositions of Direction
grammar.titleTr: Emir Cümleleri + Ne kadar + Yön Edatları
grammar.explanation: Use imperatives (commands) to give directions: Turn left, Go straight, Don't turn right. Use 'How much' to ask about prices. Use prepositions of movement (across, past, through, along, toward) to describe directions. Use this/that/these/those when pointing at things while shopping.
grammar.explanationTr: Emir cümleleri yön tarif etmek için kullanılır: Turn left (Sola dön), Go straight (Düz git), Don't turn right (Sağa dönme). 'How much' ile fiyat sorulur. Hareket edatları (across, past, through, along, toward) yön tarifinde kullanılır. Alışverişte bir şeyi gösterirken this/that/these/those kullanılır.
table: Don't turn right here, go straight.
The shop is past the traffic lights.
Go along this road for five minutes.

tips.en: Imperatives have no subject — just start with the verb: Go! Turn! Cross!
tips.tr: Emir cümlelerinde özne yok — direkt fiille başla: Go! Turn! Cross!

tips.en: Negative imperative: Don't + verb → Don't go, Don't turn
tips.tr: Olumsuz emir: Don't + fiil → Don't go (Gitme), Don't turn (Dönme)

tips.en: How much is + singular: How much is this shirt?
tips.tr: How much is + tekil: Bu gömlek ne kadar?

tips.en: How much are + plural: How much are these pants?
tips.tr: How much are + çoğul: Bu pantolonlar ne kadar?

tips.en: this/that = tekil (this shirt, that bag) — these/those = çoğul (these shoes, those socks)
tips.tr: this = bu (yakın tekil), that = şu (uzak tekil), these = bunlar (yakın çoğul), those = şunlar (uzak çoğul)

tips.en: toward = in the direction of → Walk toward the mall.
tips.tr: toward = yönüne doğru → AVM'ye doğru yürü.

reading.text:
Last Saturday, I went shopping in New York City with my friend Sarah. We took the subway to Times Square and walked toward Fifth Avenue. There are so many stores there!

First, we went to a big mall. We took the escalator to the second floor. Sarah wanted to buy a dress. She found a nice blue dress and tried it on. It fit perfectly! The dress was sixty dollars, but there was a twenty percent discount. So she paid only forty-eight dollars. She paid by credit card and got the receipt.

Then, I wanted to buy sneakers. A shop assistant helped me. I asked, "How much are these sneakers?" She said, "They are ninety dollars." That was too expensive! But she told me there was a sale on another pair — only fifty-five dollars. I tried them on and they fit great. I paid in cash and got my change.

After shopping, we were hungry. We asked a man on the street, "Excuse me, is there a good restaurant near here?" He said, "Go straight for two blocks, then turn left. There's an Italian restaurant next to the bookstore." We followed his directions and found it easily. The food was delicious!

Shopping in New York is expensive but really fun. I love the big sales!
reading.textTr:
Geçen Cumartesi, arkadaşım Sarah ile New York'ta alışverişe gittim. Metroyla Times Square'e gidip Fifth Avenue'ye doğru yürüdük. Orada çok fazla mağaza var!

Önce büyük bir AVM'ye gittik. Yürüyen merdivenle ikinci kata çıktık. Sarah bir elbise almak istiyordu. Güzel mavi bir elbise buldu ve denedi. Tam oldu! Elbise altmış dolardı ama yüzde yirmi indirim vardı. Böylece sadece kırk sekiz dolar ödedi. Kredi kartıyla ödedi ve fişi aldı.

Sonra ben spor ayakkabı almak istedim. Bir mağaza görevlisi yardım etti. "Bu spor ayakkabılar ne kadar?" diye sordum. "Doksan dolar" dedi. Çok pahalıydı! Ama başka bir çiftte indirim olduğunu söyledi — sadece elli beş dolar. Denedim ve harika oldu. Nakit ödedim ve para üstümü aldım.

Alışverişten sonra acıktık. Sokakta bir adama sorduk, "Affedersiniz, yakınlarda iyi bir restoran var mı?" "İki blok düz gidin, sonra sola dönün. Kitapçının yanında bir İtalyan restoranı var" dedi. Tarifini takip ettik ve kolayca bulduk. Yemekler çok lezzetliydi!

New York'ta alışveriş pahalı ama çok eğlenceli. Büyük indirimleri seviyorum!

questions.question: How did Eyüp and Sarah get to Times Square?
questions.questionTr: Eyüp ve Sarah Times Square'e nasıl gitti?

questions.question: How much did Sarah pay for the dress after the discount?
questions.questionTr: Sarah indirimden sonra elbiseye ne kadar ödedi?

questions.question: Why didn't Eyüp buy the first pair of sneakers?
questions.questionTr: Eyüp ilk spor ayakkabıyı neden almadı?

questions.question: How did the man say to get to the restaurant?
questions.questionTr: Adam restorana nasıl gidileceğini nasıl tarif etti?
options: Go straight for two blocks, then turn left
Cross the street and turn right

dialog.title: Lost in the City & Shopping at a Store
dialog.context: Eyüp New York'ta yol tarifi soruyor, sonra bir mağazada alışveriş yapıyor.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Excuse me, sir. I'm looking for the Central Mall. Can you help me?
lines.textTr: Affedersiniz, beyefendi. Central AVM'yi arıyorum. Yardımcı olabilir misiniz?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Sure! Go straight for two blocks, then turn right at the corner.
lines.textTr: Tabii! İki blok düz gidin, sonra köşeden sağa dönün.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Two blocks straight and then right. Got it. Is it far?
lines.textTr: İki blok düz, sonra sağ. Anladım. Uzak mı?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: No, it's about five minutes. You'll see it on your left, opposite the park.
lines.textTr: Hayır, yaklaşık beş dakika. Solunuzda göreceksiniz, parkın karşısında.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Opposite the park. Great, thank you so much!

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: You're welcome! Have a good day.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: You too! ... (Eyüp arrives at the mall and enters a shoe store)
lines.textTr: Size de! ... (Eyüp AVM'ye varıyor ve bir ayakkabı mağazasına giriyor)

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Hi there! Welcome to ShoeMart. Can I help you?
lines.textTr: Merhaba! ShoeMart'a hoş geldiniz. Yardımcı olabilir miyim?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Hi! Yes, I'm looking for sneakers. How much are those white ones?
lines.textTr: Merhaba! Evet, spor ayakkabı arıyorum. Şu beyazlar ne kadar?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Those are eighty-five dollars. But today we have a sale — twenty percent off!
lines.textTr: Onlar seksen beş dolar. Ama bugün indirim var — yüzde yirmi!

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Nice! Can I try them on? I'm size forty-two.
lines.textTr: Güzel! Deneyebilir miyim? Kırk iki numara giyiyorum.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Of course! Here you go. The fitting area is next to the cashier.
lines.textTr: Tabii! Buyurun. Deneme alanı kasiyerin yanında.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: They fit perfectly! I'll take them. Can I pay by credit card?
lines.textTr: Tam oldu! Bunları alacağım. Kredi kartıyla ödeyebilir miyim?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Absolutely! Your total is sixty-eight dollars with the discount. Here's your receipt.
lines.textTr: Kesinlikle! İndirimle toplamınız altmış sekiz dolar. Buyurun fişiniz.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Thank you! Can I exchange them if they don't fit well at home?
lines.textTr: Teşekkürler! Evde iyi olmazsa değiştirebilir miyim?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Yes, you can exchange or get a refund within fourteen days. Just keep the receipt!
lines.textTr: Evet, on dört gün içinde değiştirip veya iade edebilirsiniz. Fişi saklayın yeter!

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Perfect! Thank you for your help, Lisa.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: You're welcome! Enjoy your new sneakers. Have a great day!
lines.textTr: Rica ederim! Yeni ayakkabılarınızın keyfini çıkarın. İyi günler!

questions.question: How does Eyüp get to the mall?
options: Go straight two blocks, then turn right
Cross the street and turn left

questions.question: How much did Eyüp pay for the sneakers?
questions.questionTr: Eyüp spor ayakkabıya ne kadar ödedi?

questions.question: How long does Eyüp have to exchange the sneakers?
questions.questionTr: Eyüp ayakkabıları ne kadar sürede değiştirebilir?

fillBlanks.sentence: The shoe store is on the second ___.
The café is ___ the bank and the pharmacy.
Take the ___ to the second floor.
There is a big ___ today — everything is cheap!

multipleChoice.question: "How much ___ these shoes?" Boşluğa ne gelir?
"Can I try ___ this dress?" Boşluğa ne gelir?
"Walk ___ the hospital and turn left." (hastaneyi geçerek)
"___ is the total?" — Toplam ne kadar?

sentenceOrder.correct: Can I try on these shoes
The mall is opposite the park
Can I pay by credit card
The café is next to the bank

translation.english: Can I pay by credit card?
acceptAlso: Can I pay by credit card
Can I pay with credit card?
Can I pay with a credit card?

translation.english: Can I try on this shirt?
acceptAlso: Can I try this shirt on?
Can I try on this shirt

translation.english: The mall is opposite the park.
acceptAlso: The mall is opposite the park
The mall is across from the park.

translation.english: There is a big sale today.
acceptAlso: There is a big sale today
I would like a refund please
